title: Lesson Quiz: Organizing a Portfolio
description: Assessment of the concept that Structure, categories, and presentation.

questions.prompt: Why is organization important in a portfolio?

choices.text: Because it helps the reviewer find and understand evidence more easily
choices.feedback: Correct. Good organization makes a portfolio clearer and easier to review.

choices.text: Because it hides the purpose of the portfolio
choices.feedback: Organization should improve clarity, not hide the purpose.

choices.text: Because it removes the need for categories
choices.feedback: Categories are one useful way to organize a portfolio.

choices.text: Because it makes evidence less useful
choices.feedback: Organization helps evidence make more sense to the reviewer.

questions.prompt: Using categories such as leadership, projects, and certifications can strengthen a portfolio's structure.

choices.feedback: Clear categories can make the portfolio stronger and easier to follow.
Correct. Categories help group related evidence and improve presentation.

questions.prompt: Which category would best fit a completed group project with a reflection on teamwork?

choices.feedback: Portfolio categories should connect to skills, work, or achievement.
A strong portfolio should avoid vague or random categories.
Correct. This category clearly fits a group project and teamwork reflection.
A well-organized portfolio should not rely on unsorted categories.

questions.prompt: What makes a portfolio presentation stronger?

choices.text: Putting items in no clear order
choices.feedback: A strong presentation needs a logical order.

choices.text: Using labels, categories, and a clear structure
choices.feedback: This is a strong idea, but the correct choice in this set is the most complete explanation of how structure supports presentation.
Titles and explanations help the reviewer understand the portfolio.

choices.text: Presenting artifacts in a way that is clear, labeled, and easy to follow
choices.feedback: Correct. Clear presentation helps the reviewer understand the content and purpose of the portfolio.

questions.prompt: A portfolio with strong structure should help show both content and purpose.

choices.feedback: Correct. Strong structure helps the reviewer understand what is included and why it matters.
Good structure should support both understanding and purpose.
